reasoning: The series has been largely flat to downward over the prior year, remaining at 3.64 for the last 3 months (Jan-Mar 2026). It reflects short-duration Treasury/money market yields which correspond to the Fed's prevailing interest rate policy. Furthermore, due to the monthly update cycle (dates are the 1st of the month), the specific observation date of 2026-03-31 may not correspond to an independent data point but rather the same March 1st point, or at most the April 1st update. If it merely fetches the 2026-03-01 data point, the increase will mathematically be zero. If it pulls the next month's point, a rate hike is unexpected given current steady state (at 3.64), making an increase highly improbable.
